rescue_reasoning: The action is explicitly centered on changing resident behavior around waste handling: “Launch outreach and education campaigns to inform and support residents about waste management… [and] how to properly dispose of waste.” This supports waste diversion/correct disposal (canonical mechanism given), which is a standard mitigation pathway via reduced landfill emissions, even though no explicit GHG/emissions token appears (the stated gate failure). Climate screen = explicit_self, so retain in the climate dataset and keep primary category Mitigation.
policy_statement: Launch waste outreach and education campaigns to inform and support residents about waste management (programs available and proper disposal), including conducting needs assessments in 2020 and monitoring and reassessing campaigns.
mechanism_description: Launch outreach and education campaigns (informed by needs assessments) to encourage resident behavior change on proper waste disposal and participation in waste programs, increasing diversion and reducing landfill impacts.
primary_causal_pathway: Behavior change (waste sorting/participation) → ↑ diversion & correct disposal → ↓ landfilled organics/↑ recycling & composting → ↓ landfill methane + ↓ upstream production emissions → ↓ GHG emissions
causal_mechanism_detail:
1) Changed human activity/system: Residents’ and businesses’ waste-handling behaviors (sorting, set-out, contamination reduction, use of recycling/organics/hazardous waste programs) via outreach, education, and feedback informed by needs assessments.  
2) Mediating physical/ecological process: Less biodegradable material is anaerobically decomposed in landfills (lower CH₄ generation), and more material is routed to recycling/composting systems (displacing energy- and emissions-intensive virgin material production; reducing contamination improves recycling yields).  
3) Climate-relevant outcome: Net reduction in lifecycle GHG emissions, typically dominated by avoided landfill methane from organics diversion (and secondarily by avoided upstream CO₂ from material substitution).
dominant_pathway_test: The single biggest climate-relevant effect is reduced GHG emissions (especially avoided landfill methane) from increased waste diversion and correct disposal driven by education/outreach.
mechanism_classification_reasoning:
1) Dominant causal pathway: Outreach/education → behavior change → increased diversion/correct disposal → reduced landfill methane and other lifecycle emissions → mitigation.  
2) Why this primary over alternatives: While improved sorting can be framed as “resource efficiency,” the dominant climate-relevant endpoint across policies using this mechanism is reduced GHG emissions (not simply reduced material use or hazard resilience), so Mitigation is primary under the dominant pathway rule.  
3) Edge cases applied: If education focuses mainly on litter reduction/aesthetics or non-climate toxics compliance, climate benefits may be minor; however, the stated mechanism explicitly targets diversion and landfill impacts, keeping the dominant pathway mitigation-focused.  
4) Applicability to ALL instances: Any policy that uses outreach/education specifically to increase diversion and correct disposal generally acts through the same emissions-reduction pathway (even if the mix of methane avoidance vs. upstream substitution varies by waste stream and local system).  
5) Secondary subtype deferred: Whether the policy should be tagged secondarily as Resource Efficiency (materials circularity) or as methane-specific mitigation depends on policy-level evidence (targeted streams, baseline landfill gas capture, local recycling markets), and is not assigned here.
mechanism_edge_case_notes: If “correct disposal” primarily means hazardous waste handling without affecting landfill tonnage/organics diversion, mitigation impact could be small and the dominant pathway could shift; similarly, if diversion routes increase emissions (e.g., long-haul transport, inefficient processing), net mitigation could be reduced, though this is atypical for the mechanism as described.
key_indicators: "Expand Education and Outreach Efforts"; "Launch outreach and education campaigns"; "inform and support residents"; "information regarding how to properly dispose of waste"; "Conduct informal needs assessments"; "marketing or social behavior change campaigns"
co_benefits: Reduced litter/illegal dumping; improved local sanitation and public health; lower household hazardous waste exposure; potential cost savings from reduced contamination and improved collection efficiency.
instance_edge_case_notes: Could be interpreted as a broader planning/strategy element due to "needs assessments" and future campaign monitoring, but the core instrument described is resident-facing education/outreach (information provision).
